name: Remote: Possible Tunneling
notes: This rule triggers when possible tunneling, which can indicate a bypass of policy, or an infected system is detected.
bb_references: BB:Threats: Suspicious IP Protocol Usage: Large DNS Packets
BB:Threats: Suspicious IP Protocol Usage: Large DNS Packets match at least 10 times with the same Destination IP
BB:Threats: Suspicious IP Protocol Usage: Large ICMP Packets
conditions_text: when the flow context is Local to Local

when BB:Threats: Suspicious IP Protocol Usage: Large ICMP Packets, BB:Threats: Suspicious IP Protocol Usage: Large DNS Packets match at least 10 times with the same Destination IP, Source IP in 10 minutes
when a flow matches all of the following BB:CategoryDefinition: Successful Communication
thresholds: when BB:Threats: Suspicious IP Protocol Usage: Large ICMP Packets, BB:Threats: Suspicious IP Protocol Usage: Large DNS Packets match at least 10 times with the same Destination IP, Source IP in 10 minutes
TD_list: APPLY Remote: Possible Tunneling on flows which are detected by the LOCAL system
AND NOT when the flow context is Local to Local

AND when BB:Threats: Suspicious IP Protocol Usage: Large ICMP Packets, BB:Threats: Suspicious IP Protocol Usage: Large DNS Packets match at least 10 times with the same Destination IP, Source IP in 10 minutes
AND when a flow matches all of the following BB:CategoryDefinition: Successful Communication
RED_list: Event description: Detects when possible tunneling, which can indicate a bypass of policy, or an infected system is observed.
Force the dispatched event to create a NEW offense, select the offense using Source IP
This information should contribute to the name of the associated offense(s)
